title: DNA quantitative assay using Fluorometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5biq3","text":"The protocol follow the instruction manual from BIO-RAD of Catalog number 170-2480","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Preparation of dye solutions: Dilute the 10 mg/ml hoechst 33258 to 2 ug/ml for following steps, the ratio for dilution can follow the table: 1 mg/ml Hoechst 33258 10 mg/ml hoechst 33258 10 ul sterile water 90 ul 2 ug/ml Hoechst 33258 1 mg/ml hoechst 33258 6 ul 10x TEN assay buffer 30 ul sterile water 270 ul The mixture of 1 mg/ml Hoechst should be store in the dark at 4 degree, and the 2 ug/ml one should be make fresh before use and place in teh dark

Preparation of DNA standard: 1) Diulte the 1 mg/ml calf thymus DNA to 100 ug/ml and 10 ug/ml respectively, the ratio for dilution can follow the table: 100 ug/ml DNA standard 1 mg/ml calf thymus DNA 10 ul 10x TEN assay buffer 10 ul sterile water 80 ul 10 ug/ml DNA standard 1 mg/ml calf thymus DNA 10 ul 10x TEN assay buffer 100 ul sterile water 890 ul The mixture of DNA standard should be stored at 4 degree

Loading the microplate 1) Pipet 200 ul Hoechst 33258 dye to each well; 2) Add different amount calf thymus DNA to wells and mix the solutions using a disposable transfer pipet for each well; 3)Add the sample into wells and mix the solutions well 4) Measure the fluorescence using an excitation filter of 360-390nm and an emission filter of 450-470nm . a
creator: Xueying Zhong
